title: How to Report Cybercrime
title_hi: साइबर अपराध की शिकायत कैसे करें
description: Step-by-step guide to reporting online fraud, hacking, identity theft, and other cybercrimes

steps.details: For financial fraud (UPI/net banking/credit card), call 1930 within the first hour. Quick reporting increases chances of recovering your money. The helpline operates 24/7.
Call your bank's customer care. Request blocking of your account/card/UPI. File a dispute for unauthorized transactions. Banks must resolve disputes within 90 days.
Visit cybercrime.gov.in and select complaint category: Financial Fraud, Women/Child Related, or Other Cybercrimes. Create an account and file your complaint with all evidence.
Do NOT delete any messages, emails, or call records from the scammer. Take screenshots of everything. Save URLs, phone numbers, UPI IDs, and account numbers used by the scammer.

steps.action: File an FIR at the local police station
steps.details: Visit your nearest police station with all evidence. File an FIR under IT Act sections 66C (identity theft), 66D (online fraud), or other relevant sections. Get a copy of the FIR.
If the fraud involves unauthorized banking transactions, also file a complaint with RBI through their Banking Ombudsman scheme at cms.rbi.org.in.
Track your complaint on cybercrime.gov.in using your complaint number. Follow up with the police. Contact the bank's nodal officer if the dispute is not resolved.

required_documents: Bank account statements showing unauthorized transactions
tips: Report financial fraud within 1 hour for best chance of recovery
NEVER share OTP, PIN, or password with anyone claiming to be from your bank
Banks will NEVER ask for your OTP or password over phone
Save ALL evidence — do not delete messages from scammers
Change all passwords for compromised accounts immediately
Enable two-factor authentication on all accounts
Check if your money can be frozen using the 1930 helpline
